summary: codebuff-cli impersonates CodebuffAI and runs an unverified binary from an unrelated GitHub account with TLS verification disabled
details: The package name and README impersonate the legitimate CodebuffAI `codebuff` CLI (README links to codebuff.com and CodebuffAI/codebuff and instructs `npm install -g codebuff`), but the package is published from an unrelated personal account. When the installed `codebuff` bin is invoked, `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` queries `api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest`, downloads `codebuff-<platform>` from that release, chmods it 0755, caches it under `~/.codebuff/bin/`, and execs it. The source URL is the mutable `latest` tag on a personal GitHub repo that does not match the impersonated publisher, and no hash or signature is verified — the maintainer can change the executed bytes at any time without republishing the npm package. The wrapper then spawns the downloaded binary with `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED='0'` injected into its environment (cli/bin/codebuff.cjs line 184), disabling TLS certificate validation for any HTTPS the binary performs and exposing whatever credentials or code it fetches to interception.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
